content: Hey, I'm thinking of renovating my home studio in LA. I want to soundproof it and get some new equipment.
That sounds like a great project! What kind of equipment are you looking to get?
I'm thinking of getting a new mixer and some monitors. I've been doing some research and I'm considering the Yamaha MG12XU mixer.
The Yamaha MG12XU is a great choice. Have you thought about how you're going to soundproof your studio?

Yeah, I've been looking into it. I think I'm going to use acoustic panels and some mass loaded vinyl. I've also been volunteering at the animal shelter on weekends, so I've been talking to some of the other volunteers who have experience with soundproofing.
That's really great that you're volunteering at the animal shelter. It's always good to give back to the community. Do you have any experience with soundproofing yourself?
No, not really. But I've been doing some research and I think I can handle it. I've also been running a lot lately, I just completed my 10th marathon last month in San Francisco.
Wow, congratulations on completing your 10th marathon! That's a great achievement. Do you think you'll be able to find time to work on your studio with your volunteering and running schedule?
Yeah, I think so. I'm pretty busy, but I'm also really passionate about music. I've been producing and DJing for years, and I'm excited to get my new studio up and running.
It's great that you're passionate about music. What kind of music do you typically produce and DJ?
I mostly produce and DJ electronic music. I've been playing at some local clubs in LA and I'm hoping to start playing at some bigger venues soon.
That's really cool. Have you thought about how you're going to market yourself to get more gigs?
Yeah, I've been thinking about it. I'm planning on creating a website and social media accounts to promote myself and my music.
That's a great idea. Do you have any experience with web design or social media marketing?
No, not really. But I'm willing to learn. I've also been brewing my own beer at home, so I'm not afraid to try new things.
Home brewing is a great hobby. Maybe you can even use your music studio to host beer tasting events or something.
Ha! That's not a bad idea. I'll have to think about it. Thanks for the chat, it's been helpful to talk through my ideas.
No problem, it was great chatting with you. Good luck with your studio renovation and your music career!
